name: Foundations of Pharmacovigilance in the AI Era
definition: Understanding of the historical context, public health importance, and basic concepts of pharmacovigilance, integrating traditional principles with emerging AI applications.

name: Clinical Aspects of ADRs with AI-Enhanced Analysis
definition: Knowledge of the classification, mechanisms, risk factors, and clinical management of adverse drug reactions, with understanding of AI applications for enhanced detection and assessment.

name: Important ADRs and Their Recognition Using AI
definition: Knowledge of specific adverse reaction patterns affecting various organ systems and the application of AI for enhanced recognition and prediction.

name: Individual Case Safety Reports (ICSRs) in the AI Era
definition: Understanding and application of processes for collecting, evaluating, and reporting individual adverse event cases with AI-augmented capabilities.

name: Pharmacovigilance in Clinical Trials with AI Integration
definition: Knowledge of safety monitoring in clinical trials and the application of AI to enhance trial safety surveillance.

name: Medication Errors, Quality Issues and AI Detection
definition: Understanding of medication error patterns, product quality concerns, and AI applications for detection and prevention.

name: Spontaneous Reporting Systems with AI Enhancement
definition: Knowledge of database systems, processes, and analytics for managing spontaneous adverse event reports with AI enhancements.

name: Signal Detection and Management Using AI
definition: Understanding methods for identifying, validating, and evaluating potential safety signals with AI-enhanced approaches.

name: Post-Authorization Observational Studies and Clinical Trials in PV
definition: Knowledge of study designs and methodologies for evaluating safety in the post-marketing environment with AI applications.

name: Benefit-Risk Assessment in the AI Era
definition: Understanding approaches for evaluating and balancing benefits and risks of medications with AI-enhanced capabilities.

name: Risk Management with AI Integration
definition: Knowledge of strategies and tools for minimizing identified risks with AI applications for enhanced effectiveness.

name: Industry and Regulatory Authorities, Mandatory Procedures from Legislation
definition: Understanding regulatory requirements, industry obligations, and inspection processes related to pharmacovigilance.

name: Global Pharmacovigilance and Public Health with AI Support
definition: Knowledge of international pharmacovigilance systems, public health integration, and AI applications for global safety monitoring.

name: Communication in the AI Era
definition: Understanding principles, channels, and strategies for effective safety communication with AI enhancements.

name: Sources of Information in the AI Era
definition: Knowledge of data sources, retrieval methods, and evaluation approaches for pharmacovigilance information with AI enhancements.
